summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is 'Customer Acquisition Cost' (CAC)?

Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) is the total amount spent on marketing and sales divided by the number of new customers gained during that period. It measures the efficiency of customer acquisition efforts.

A business has a 30% gross margin and spends $6,000 on a marketing campaign. What is the break-even revenue needed from this campaign?

Break-even revenue = Marketing spend / Gross margin = $6,000 / 0.30 = $20,000. Each dollar of revenue only generates $0.30 in gross profit, so $20,000 in revenue is needed to cover the $6,000 cost.

A retail store increases its average order value from $35 to $42 by adding a 'frequently bought together' feature. With 3,000 monthly orders, what is the monthly revenue increase?

AOV increase = $42 - $35 = $7 per order. Monthly revenue increase = $7 x 3,000 orders = $21,000. This is a no-cost revenue increase since it simply optimizes existing traffic.

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: If each customer spends $30/month for an average of 12 months, LTV =

LTV = Average revenue per month x Average customer lifespan = $30 x 12 = $360.

Example: What does 'average order value' (AOV) mean?

Average Order Value (AOV) = Total revenue / Number of orders. Increasing AOV (through upselling, bundling, or minimum order incentives) is a cost-effective way to grow revenue without acquiring more customers.

Example: A retail store increases its average order value from $35 to $42 by adding a 'frequently bought together' feature. With 3,000 monthly orders, what is the monthly revenue increase?

AOV increase = $42 - $35 = $7 per order. Monthly revenue increase = $7 x 3,000 orders = $21,000. This is a no-cost revenue increase since it simply optimizes existing traffic.
